response.justification: The candidate is a strong, well-rounded fullstack engineer whose TypeScript, Node.js, React, AWS, Kubernetes, and even LLM/MCP tooling experience covers essentially every required and nice-to-have skill. However, with roughly 19 years of experience and current Staff-level seniority, he is dramatically overqualified for an explicitly entry-level position, raising real concerns about salary alignment, engagement with junior-scoped work, and retention. He is Berlin-based, which fits the high on-site requirement, so an interview could be worthwhile if he genuinely wants this role level.
strengths: Extensive TypeScript/Node.js and React fullstack experience directly matching the core stack
Covers nearly all nice-to-haves: Kubernetes, AWS, and recent LLM/MCP agent-tooling work
Strong software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, testing) aligned with clean-architecture emphasis
concerns: Severely overqualified for an entry-level role, posing salary, motivation, and retention risks
No explicit PostgreSQL or Nest.js experience (MongoDB/MySQL and other frameworks instead)
Only basic German, which may hinder work in a German renovation/energy-consulting domain
